graphdb_rdf.metric_question: Under what conditions is a biometric categorisation system considered a 'purely ancillary feature' and therefore not subject to the rules of this Regulation?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: A biometric categorisation system is considered a "purely ancillary feature" and therefore not subject to the rules of the EU AI Act under specific conditions. While the exact conditions are not detailed in the retrieved information, the concept of a "purely ancillary feature" generally refers to functionalities that do not serve as the primary purpose of the system but rather support or complement the main service being provided. In this context, the biometric categorisation must be subordinate to a principal service, meaning that it cannot be the primary function of the system. The system must primarily serve another purpose, and the biometric categorisation should not be essential for the operation of that principal service. For further details, you may refer to the following entities: - [Purely Ancillary Feature](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Characteristic/purely_ancillary_feature) - [Biometric Categorisation Systems](https://euaiact.eu/resource/System/biometric_categorisation_systems) If you need more specific conditions or examples, please let me know!
